title: Server-Side Request Forgery via Notification and Integration URLs

cvss_v3_1.notes: PR:L because API key required. S:C because Radarr makes requests to internal services on behalf of the attacker. C:L for information disclosure from internal service responses.

description:
Radarr accepts arbitrary URLs in notification webhook settings, download client base URLs, indexer URLs, and media cover download URLs without validating the URL scheme or host. An authenticated attacker can configure integrations pointing to internal network addresses (e.g., cloud metadata endpoints at 169.254.169.254, internal APIs, localhost services).

When combined with RADARR-001 (CORS), this becomes exploitable from any website that knows the API key. The attacker creates a webhook notification via cross-origin POST, then triggers it to make Radarr send HTTP requests to internal targets.

root_cause.detail: No URL scheme or host validation. User-supplied URLs passed directly to HttpRequestBuilder without filtering private/internal addresses.

remediation: Validate webhook and integration URLs against an allowlist of schemes (http, https only)
Consider implementing URL validation as a shared service used by all integration providers
